rationales.justification: KenGen controls Kenya's geothermal resources which are a national strategic asset. Government ownership ensures energy security and affordable electricity for economic development under Kenya Vision 2030.
rationales.publicInterestRationale: Energy security and affordable electricity access
rationales.reviewOutcome: Retain majority; NSE listing maintained at 30%

rationales.justification: National carrier essential for tourism, trade, and connectivity. Government ownership prevents loss of landing rights and bilateral air service agreements that are tied to national carrier status.
rationales.publicInterestRationale: National connectivity, tourism, and trade facilitation
rationales.reviewOutcome: Retain 48.9%; explore strategic investor for additional equity

rationales.justification: Electricity distribution is a natural monopoly. Government ownership ensures universal service obligations are met, including subsidised tariffs for low-income consumers.
rationales.publicInterestRationale: Universal electricity access and consumer protection
rationales.reviewOutcome: Retain majority; consider private sector participation in distribution

rationales.justification: Port infrastructure is a natural monopoly with significant national security implications. Government ownership ensures port revenues benefit the public and strategic cargo handling is maintained.
rationales.publicInterestRationale: Trade facilitation, national security, and regional integration
rationales.reviewOutcome: Retain 100%; consider terminal concessions to private operators

rationales.justification: Private sector unwilling to invest in rail infrastructure given high capital costs and long payback periods. Government ownership necessary to maintain SGR operations and service the Chinese debt.
rationales.publicInterestRationale: Transport infrastructure, debt management, and regional connectivity
rationales.reviewOutcome: Retain 100%; explore PPP for operations management

rationales.justification: Pipeline infrastructure is critical for national energy security. However, commercial operations can be partially privatised while government retains strategic control.
rationales.publicInterestRationale: Energy security and petroleum supply chain reliability
rationales.reviewOutcome: Partial privatisation planned for 2025; government to retain 51%

policyAlignments.specificGoal: Increase electricity generation capacity to 23,000 MW by 2030
Tourism — increase tourist arrivals to 5 million annually
policyAlignments.measurableOutcome: KQ carries 35% of international tourists arriving in Kenya

policyAlignments.specificGoal: Position Kenya as East Africa's logistics hub
policyAlignments.measurableOutcome: Mombasa Port handles 35M tonnes; LAPSSET Phase 1 operational
SGR operational but financially unsustainable; debt burden constrains expansion

communityServiceObligations.description: Subsidised electricity tariff for consumers using below 50 kWh per month, protecting low-income households from full cost-reflective tariffs
Free-to-air broadcasting of government communications, educational content, and public health messages in 20+ languages including Swahili, English, and vernacular languages
Below-cost commuter rail services on the Nairobi Commuter Rail network, providing affordable transport for Nairobi residents
